assertion_results.text: Description includes a Summary section with a 1-3 sentence overview mentioning OAuth2 or Google login
assertion_results.evidence: First line: 'This PR adds Google OAuth2 login to the application'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Motivation section explaining WHY (user feedback, 60% requested social login)
assertion_results.evidence: No motivation section. Jumps from summary to file list.

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Changes section that groups changes by concept (auth flow, data model, configuration) rather than listing files one by one
assertion_results.evidence: File-by-file list with no conceptual grouping

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Testing Instructions section with numbered, copy-pasteable steps for verifying the OAuth flow
assertion_results.evidence: Says 'Run the tests with npm test' — not numbered, not specific

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Rollback Plan section that addresses the database migration (ALTER TABLE) and how to reverse it
Description includes a Reviewer Notes section that flags security-sensitive areas (token handling, OAuth callback, session management)
assertion_results.evidence: Notes section has no security guidance

assertion_results.text: Testing instructions include at least one edge case or error scenario (e.g., user denies consent, invalid token, duplicate Google account)
Rollback plan mentions the database migration specifically and whether it is reversible
